summary: High‑energy ’80s show with blazing Jerry, crisp Brent keyboards and generally good soundboard capture despite occasional mud and splices.
review: The performance is praised for its nonstop drive, standout China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, Cumberland and a fiery Stella Blue, plus an incendiary Estimated/Going‑Down‑the‑Road sequence. Reviewers note the soundboard source is mostly clear with Brent’s keys shining, though one finds it “muddy” and another points out a spliced transition that detracts from an otherwise solid recording.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars highlighting strong musical moments; only a few note recording flaws, yielding an overall high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear with crisp vocals and Brent’s keyboards; some listeners report muddy mids, loss of fidelity after tape transfer and a noticeable splice in the transition.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Shows a relentless, high‑energy flow from start to finish; a few reviewers call the set uneven but agree the peaks (especially after the spliced transition) are worth hearing. The era’s uptempo feel and occasional jam mishaps add character rather than detract.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly described as ‘on fire’, blazing, high‑energy and occasionally stumbling on lyrics but still delivering powerful solos.
band_member_comments.Phil: One reviewer wonders where Phil is in the mix; no specific performance comments.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work is highlighted as crisp, clear and a defining element of this era.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Rhythm Devils are noted for speeding up the pace after an inspired Garcia solo, adding drive to the show.
